expectations.text: A0: Agent produced both output files with real content
expectations.evidence: quality-scenarios.md (152 lines, 17 scenarios) and analysis.md (51 lines) both present with substantive content.

expectations.text: A1: Scenarios use ATAM format (Source, Stimulus, Environment, Artifact, Response, Response Measure)
expectations.evidence: All 17 scenarios have all 6 ATAM fields: Source, Stimulus, Artifact, Environment, Response, Response Measure.

expectations.text: A2: Diverse test types — at least 3 different types, not all fitness-functions
expectations.evidence: No test types are assigned to scenarios at all. The scenarios do not include a 'Test Type' field. There is no classification of scenarios into unit-test, integration-test, load-test, chaos-test, fitness-function, or manual-review.

expectations.text: A3: Style fitness functions NOT duplicated — no scenarios for structural invariants already covered by style FFs
expectations.evidence: QS-SCALE-02 (Independent Service Scaling) tests that 'Catalog service scales independently without affecting other services' — this overlaps with the service boundary alignment style FF. QS-DEPLOY-02 (Independent Service Deployment) tests 'Only the Catalog service is deployed; no other services require redeployment' — this is essentially the service boundary enforcement style FF. QS-TEST-01 (Code Coverage Threshold) duplicates the existing >80% coverage fitness function mentioned in architecture.md.

expectations.text: A4: Tradeoffs identified — at least 1 explicit tension/conflict between characteristics
expectations.evidence: The analysis.md mentions 'Gaps and Recommendations' but does not identify any explicit tradeoffs or tensions between characteristics. No conflicts documented.

expectations.text: A5: Summary table present — table listing all scenarios with test types
expectations.evidence: The analysis.md has a scenario distribution table by characteristic, but it does not list individual scenarios with their test types. The quality-scenarios.md has no summary table at all.

expectations.text: A6: Response measures are CONCRETE — numbers, thresholds, or specific observable outcomes
expectations.evidence: All measures are concrete: 'p95 latency < 100ms' (QS-PERF-01), '99.99% uptime measured monthly' (QS-AVAIL-01), 'Zero PCI-DSS compliance violations in quarterly audits' (QS-SEC-01), 'within 60 seconds' (QS-SCALE-02), etc.
